body:
Title: CRA Form T2203 2025 Nova Scotia and New Brunswick multiple-jurisdiction tax.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t2203/t2203-25e.pdf

CRA T2203 2025 Form NS428MJ calculates Nova Scotia tax for multiple jurisdictions. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 0.0879, 0.1495, 0.1667, 0.175, 0.21, 0.575, 0.05, 0.75, 0.25, and 0.20. NS428MJ tax brackets use thresholds $30,507, $61,015, $95,883, and $154,650; rates 8.79%, 14.95%, 16.67%, 17.5%, and 21%; and base amounts $0.00, $2,681.57, $7,242.51, $13,055.01, and $23,339.23. Lines 11 to 16 add unallocated tax on split income and subtract non-refundable tax credits, dividend tax credit, and minimum tax carryover at 57.5%. Lines 17 to 20 add additional tax for minimum tax purposes at 57.5% and multiply by the NS allocation percentage. For residents, lines 21 to 28 subtract the resident adjustment based on selected Part 3 credit lines at 8.79%, dividend tax credit, and the percentage of income not allocated to NS. Lines 31 to 33 subtract provincial foreign tax credit and add Nova Scotia research and development tax credit recapture. Lines 34 to 39 calculate adjusted family income. Lines 41 to 52 calculate the low-income tax reduction: $300 basic reduction, $300 spouse reduction, $300 eligible dependant reduction, maximum $600 before dependent children, $165 per dependent child, $15,000 base amount, and 5% reduction rate. Lines 55 to 68 subtract a 75% political contribution credit capped at $750, a 25% food bank tax credit for farmers, a 20% labour-sponsored venture-capital tax credit capped at $2,000, equity tax credit, innovation equity tax credit, venture capital tax credit, and the $1,000 age tax credit, then enter Nova Scotia tax on Part 5 line 3.

CRA T2203 2025 Form NB428MJ calculates New Brunswick tax for multiple jurisdictions. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 0.094, 0.14, 0.16, 0.195, 0.57, and 0.03. NB428MJ tax brackets use thresholds $51,306, $102,614, and $190,060; rates 9.40%, 14%, 16%, and 19.50%; and base amounts $0.00, $4,822.76, $12,005.88, and $25,997.24. Lines 11 to 16 add unallocated tax on split income and subtract non-refundable tax credits, dividend tax credit, and minimum tax carryover at 57%. Lines 17 to 20 add additional tax for minimum tax purposes at 57% and multiply by the NB allocation percentage. For residents, lines 21 to 28 subtract the resident adjustment based on line 58360 at 9.4%, dividend tax credit, and the percentage of income not allocated to NB. Line 30 subtracts provincial foreign tax credit. Lines 34 to 39 calculate adjusted family income. The low-income tax reduction is available if a single taxpayer has net income under $48,653 or a taxpayer with a spouse or eligible dependant has adjusted family income under $75,387. Lines 41 to 50 calculate the low-income tax reduction: $802 basic reduction, $802 spouse reduction, $802 eligible dependant reduction, maximum $1,604, $21,920 base amount, and 3% reduction rate. Lines 52 to 62 subtract low-income reduction, political contribution tax credit, labour-sponsored venture capital fund tax credit capped at $2,000, and small business investor tax credit capped at $125,000, then enter New Brunswick tax on Part 5 line 4.
